notes: Frozen large_model_value_v0 strategy definition and golden decisions for issue #21. Golden inputs are grounded in the checked-in sample corpus under apps/data-engine/samples/; every expected number is recomputed with Decimal arithmetic by the tests. Golden decisions are engineering regression anchors over a fixed research cohort, not performance claims. Tier bands, the confidence floor, and the per-cutoff 3-month T-bill rates are provisional versioned research parameters pending calibration. Decision evaluation order: missing required inputs, then the consumed-confidence floor, then the uniform capital-adjusted factor computation with the tier-band valuation check, then ranking by descending valuation gap and top-N equal-weight selection. The v0 formula is uniform across every issuer (2026-07-18 owner decision): financial issuers take the identical (gross_profit - total_assets*risk_free_rate)/headcount definition, with gross_profit supplied by the parser's financial_issuer_split pre-provision-profit proxy. There is no financial-sector branch or blanket exclusion.

decisions.derivation: capital_charge = 52389000000 * 0.0380 = 1990782000.0000; adjusted_gross_profit = 5033000000 - 1990782000.0000 = 3042218000.0000; capital_adjusted_gppe = 3042218000.0000 / 41496 = 73313.52 (2dp half-even) -> traditional tier; market_value = 481201844 * 72.69 = 34978562040.36; current_ps = 34978562040.36 / 80269000000 = 0.4358 (4dp half-even); target_ps = (0.30 + 2.00) / 2 = 1.1500; valuation_gap = 1.1500 / 0.4358 - 1 = 1.6388 (4dp half-even); ranked 1 of 2 eligible by descending gap (issuer:nice gap 0.8788); top-2 selected; target_weight = 1/2 = 0.500000 (6dp half-even)

inputs.grounding: apps/data-engine/samples/filings/JPM_10K_000162828026008131.html (FY2025 10-K, filed 2026-02-13): 'As of December 31, 2025, JPMorganChase had 318,512 employees globally'
apps/data-engine/samples/prices/JPM_prices_3y_20260712.csv row 2026-03-31 unadjusted Close 294.16 (immutable 2026-07-12 Yahoo chart capture)
apps/data-engine/samples/sec/JPM_CIK0000019617.json us-gaap:Revenues FY2025 (2025-01-01..2025-12-31), filed 2026-02-13
apps/data-engine/samples/sec/JPM_CIK0000019617.json dei:EntityCommonStockSharesOutstanding = 2697032375 as of 2026-01-31, filed 2026-02-13 (FY2025 10-K cover page)
apps/data-engine/samples/sec/JPM_CIK0000019617.json us-gaap:Assets as of 2025-12-31, filed 2026-02-13

apps/data-engine/samples/sec/JPM_CIK0000019617.json: bank pre-provision-profit proxy (metrics.py financial_issuer_split) = us-gaap:InterestIncomeExpenseNet (95443000000) + us-gaap:NoninterestIncome (87004000000) - us-gaap:NoninterestExpense (95640000000) = 86807000000, FY2025 (2025-01-01..2025-12-31), filed 2026-02-13

decisions.derivation: JPM takes the identical uniform v0 formula (2026-07-18 owner decision): capital_charge = total_assets * risk_free_rate; capital_adjusted_labor_efficiency = (gross_profit - capital_charge) / headcount, where gross_profit is the parser's financial_issuer_split pre-provision-profit proxy (95443000000 + 87004000000 - 95640000000 = 86807000000). A 4.4T balance sheet drives the capital charge above pre-provision profit, so real profit and thus labor efficiency are negative -- a valid low signal, not a special exclusion. Negative labor efficiency maps to the traditional tier (open lower bound); JPM's current P/S sits above the traditional target band upper bound (2.00), so the decision is rejected_valuation_above_tier_band exactly like any other over-valued issuer.
